description_long: This mud patch is an irregular, roughly rectangular clump of wet earth with a rugged, uneven surface. The texture is highly varied, featuring small craters, shallow depressions, and scattered protrusions, likely from embedded stones or debris. The coloration is predominantly a mix of dark brown and gray, with some lighter patches suggesting drier or more compacted areas. A few small, rounded objects, possibly pebbles or bits of litter, are partially embedded in the mud, adding to its naturalistic appearance. The edges of the patch are jagged and blend unevenly into the surrounding ground, with no sharp boundary. The surface appears moist and sticky, with a matte, non-reflective finish.
description: An irregular, moist mud patch with a rugged, cratered surface and scattered embedded debris. The edges are jagged and blend naturally into the ground.
description_view_0: From this view, the mud patch appears elongated with several shallow craters and a few embedded objects visible on the surface.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the patch's thickness and shows a prominent rounded object near the edge, with more visible surface undulations.
description_view_2: Seen from this side, the patch looks flatter and the surface texture appears more granular, with the largest dimension visible.
description_view_3: This view emphasizes the irregular, jagged edge and shows a more compacted, less cratered surface area.
attribute_boundary_definition: The boundaries are irregular and blend gradually into the surrounding ground, with no sharp transitions.
